body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 49/2026
Made under: Taxation (Rural Area) Act
Published in: No. 6 – B.C. Regs. 43 to 56, April 7, 2026
Deposit date: April 7, 2026

B.C. Reg. 49/2026, deposited April 7, 2026, under theTAXATION (RURAL AREA) ACT[section 20]. Order in Council 125/2026, approved and ordered April 7, 2026. On the recommendation of the undersigned, the Administrator, by and with the advice and consent of the Executive Council, orders that the Taxation (Rural Area) ActRegulation,B.C. Reg. 387/82, is amended as set out in the attached Schedule. — B. BAILEY,Minister of Finance; R. PARMAR,Presiding Member of the Executive Council. 1	Sections 5 and 6 of the Taxation (Rural Area) Act Regulation, B.C. Reg. 387/82, are repealed and the following substituted: 5For the purposes of section 20 of the Act, in all areas of British Columbia except the Peace River Regional District, the tax rates for 2026 and subsequent tax years are the amounts set out in column 2 of the following table to be applied against each $1 000 of actual value of property in the appropriate class set out opposite in column 1: Rate for 2026 andsubsequent years($) Recreational property/Non-profit organization Variable tax rate – Peace River Regional District 6For the purposes of section 20 of the Act, in the Peace River Regional District, the tax rates for 2026 and subsequent tax years are the amounts set out in column 2 of the following table to be applied against each $1 000 of actual value of property in the appropriate class set out opposite in column 1: Rate for 2026 andsubsequent years($) Recreational property/Non-profit organization Index Page|Previous Regulation|Next Regulation|Cumulative Index Page Copyright © 2026: King's Printer, Victoria, British Columbia, Canada

minutes_briefs.title: BC rural property tax rates updated for 2026 and beyond
minutes_briefs.summary: The BC government has updated the tax rates for recreational and non-profit organization properties in rural areas (outside municipalities) for 2026 and future years. The new rates apply to all areas of British Columbia except the Peace River Regional District, which has its own separate rate. This change affects property owners in rural areas who own recreational property or property used by a non-profit organization.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Check your 2026 property tax notice to confirm the new rate applies to your recreational or non-profit property in a rural area.

key_dates.label: New rural property tax rates for recreational/non-profit properties take effect

minutes_briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Agriculture, Natural Resources
